text.title: Template Task Worked Example
text.summary: One end-to-end worked example showing how template-task produces a valid TASK file artefact.

example.title: Happy-path application: JWT refresh endpoint
example.code:
# TASK_001: Add POST /api/v1/auth/refresh endpoint
**Feature:** jwt-refresh **Status:** todo **Created:** 2026-05-23
## SDD References
| Document | Path | |----------|------| | Spec | .aidocs/features/in-progress/jwt-refresh/spec.md | | Design | .aidocs/features/in-progress/jwt-refresh/design.md | | Plan | .aidocs/features/in-progress/jwt-refresh/implementation-plan.md |
## Requirements Coverage
### FR-3: System SHALL provide refresh-token rotation on POST /api/v1/auth/refresh. Covered: this task implements the endpoint + rotation.
### AD-2: Use HS256 with 15-min access TTL, 7-day refresh TTL. Applied: SimpleJWT config tweaks land here.
## Objective
Add POST /api/v1/auth/refresh endpoint that validates the refresh token, rotates it, and returns a new access+refresh pair.
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] AC-1.1: Given a valid refresh token / When POST /api/v1/auth/refresh / Then HTTP 200 with `{access, refresh}` body and new refresh token. - [ ] AC-1.2: Given an expired refresh token / When POST /api/v1/auth/refresh / Then HTTP 401 with error code `token_expired`. - [ ] AC-1.3: Given the same refresh token submitted twice / When POST second time / Then HTTP 401 with error code `token_reused`.
## Files
| File | Action | Scope | |------|--------|-------| | apps/auth/views/refresh.py | CREATE | View + rotation logic | | apps/auth/urls.py | MODIFY | Wire new URL | | apps/auth/tests/test_refresh.py | CREATE | Tests for AC-1.1, AC-1.2, AC-1.3 |
## Estimated Tokens
~55k total (impl ~20k, tests ~30k, SDD load ~5k).
## Implementation
<!-- Executor fills this section during execution -->
## Summary
<!-- Executor fills this section after completion -->
example.note: Demonstrates the rules from 01-core-rules.xml applied to a concrete plan row; output validates against the schema in 02-output-contract.xml.
